overview: Jeskai Artifacts is a Standard tempo deck that leverages artifact synergies to establish early board presence and generate consistent card advantage. By cycling threats through Repurposing Bay and amplifying artifact triggers with Simulacrum Synthesizer, the deck maintains pressure while protecting its resources. United Battlefront serves as a pivotal board-clearing tool to reset unfavorable states and secure late-game swings.
win_conditions: Overwhelming the opponent with repeated artifact creature triggers
Using United Battlefront to clear opposing boards while preserving your artifact synergies
Generating sustained card advantage through Repurposing Bay recursion

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more low-cost artifacts
Early interaction paired with a ramp or draw engine
Simulacrum Synthesizer alongside cheap artifact enablers

Keep hands with 2-3 lands (duals/talismans preferred), at least one of Simulacrum Synthesizer, Repurposing Bay, or United Battlefront, and 2-3 cheap instants/sorceries (e.g., Lightning Strike, Path to Exile, Consider, Opt).
Keep hands with mana rocks if paired with a turn-2 play.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifact synergy or interaction
Mulligan to pure mana rocks, multiple lands with no artifacts, artifact-heavy hands with zero instants/sorceries, or hands lacking early interaction against aggro.

early_game: Play cheap artifacts to trigger Simulacrum Synthesizer
Establish a minimal board presence to apply pressure
Use early interaction to disrupt opponent's game plan
Turn 1: Play a land and a talisman/mana rock, or cast a cheap instant/sorcery if you have free mana.
Turn 2: Deploy Simulacrum Synthesizer or an artifact engine, or cast a second cheap spell to trigger Repurposing Bay/United Battlefront.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast a turn-2 spell and a turn-3 artifact, or vice versa.
Prioritize playing duals/talismans early to enable Jeskai's two-color requirements for removal and card draw.
mid_game: Activate Repurposing Bay to recycle dead cards into threats
Scale artifact synergies to outpace opponent's development
Hold up mana for counterspells to protect key permanents
Preserve instants/sorceries for engine triggers and removal
avoid overcommitting to casting spells just to make tokens without follow-up.
Use card draw (Consider, Opt, Thoughtcast) to cycle into threats or answers.
Manage graveyard carefully if playing Karn, the Great Creator or Engineered Plated Warbeast.
Track opponent's mana to time your spells when they're tapped out.
Commit threats when you have removal or protection ready to handle interaction.
late_game: Cast United Battlefront to wipe opposing boards and swing with your artifacts
Leverage card advantage from Repurposing Bay to refill your hand
Close out games with repeated artifact triggers and combat damage
A fully fueled board of Clone tokens backed by artifact pumps (Engineered Plated Warbeast, Arcbound Ravager, Karn, the Great Creator), or a Karn Liberated / Urabrask the Hidden finish.
Simulacrum Synthesizer copying a pumped threat or Restoration Angel blinking a key artifact can close games.
Jeskai's removal can clear the path for a decisive attack.
Go all-in when you have card advantage secured, the opponent has no sweepers/counters in hand, or you've established a board state that forces them to overextend.
Commit when you can protect your threats with Lightning Greaves or Restoration Angel, and when your opponent's graveyard/mana is depleted.

key_cards.name: Simulacrum Synthesizer
key_cards.notes: Prioritize playing cheap artifacts before or alongside it to maximize trigger value and board presence.

key_cards.name: Repurposing Bay
key_cards.notes: Use it to dig for answers or recycle played artifacts back into your hand for repeated value.

key_cards.name: United Battlefront
key_cards.notes: Hold until opponent commits multiple threats, then cast to clear their board while your artifacts survive or trigger favorably.

sequencing_tips: Trigger Simulacrum Synthesizer before combat to maximize value
Activate Repurposing Bay after opponent commits resources to the board
Cast United Battlefront on your turn to immediately follow up with artifact attacks
Hold up colored mana for interaction before committing to artifact plays
Be mindful of the 'Clone' token name synergy
it doesn't interact with other token types or generic artifact synergies.
Simulacrum Synthesizer's upkeep ability is a powerful tool but requires careful mana management.
Repurposing Bay and United Battlefront trigger on any instant/sorcery, so even cheap removal or cantrips are valuable.
Avoid overcommitting to casting spells just to make tokens if you lack board presence or protection.
Watch for artifact hate (Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void, Chalice of the Void) and have a plan to pivot to non-artifact threats or direct damage if needed.

matchups.advice: Ramp quickly and use United Battlefront to reset their board while preserving your artifact triggers.
Go wide with artifacts and use Repurposing Bay to outvalue their hand traps and removal.
Race with artifact pressure and time United Battlefront to clear their value engines.

Use Chalice of the Void (if applicable), Thoughtcast to dig, or cast spells at instant speed when they're tapped out. Use Simulacrum Synthesizer ETB effect before they can react, or rely on artifact mana rocks that don't target. Cast instants/sorceries in response to their counterspells to trigger Repurposing Bay/United Battlefront before they resolve, turning their interaction into token generation.

Apply early pressure with Clone tokens. Use Path to Exile or Dovin’s Veto on key ramp pieces. Don't let them stabilize; force them to interact with your board while you develop. Use Jeskai's card draw to out-resource their acceleration and close before they hit their late-game win conditions.

Win the resource war. Prioritize Karn, the Great Creator and Engineered Plated Warbeast for value. Use Restoration Angel and Lightning Greaves to protect key artifacts. Trade efficiently and out-draw them with Consider/Opt. Control the board with Simulacrum Synthesizer copying their key threats, and use Jeskai's removal to clear blockers before attacking.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Wrath of God, Supreme Verdict, Cyclonic Rift), counterspells (Spell Pierce, Flusterstorm, Dovin’s Veto), and hate cards (Chalice of the Void, Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void).
Track opponent's mana and card count to predict when they can interact.
Note when they tap out for a single spell vs. holding up mana, and adjust your spell timing accordingly.
